name: Lovable
category: AI app builder / no-code and AI-assisted web app builder
summary: Evidence-backed Lovable research hub for NoCodeReviewed. Final review, security verdict, autonomy score, and production-readiness claims remain pending hands-on testing.
safeClaims: Lovable is positioned as an AI app builder for creating apps and websites through chat.
Lovable can be described as a prompt-driven app and website builder based on its official positioning.
Lovable supports Supabase integration for database, auth, storage, realtime, and edge functions according to official documentation.
Lovable supports GitHub sync/export for code backup, collaboration, local IDE work, and external deployment according to official documentation.
Lovable projects can be published to a live URL, with updates requiring a new publish action according to official publishing documentation.
Lovable provides public security, privacy, and compliance documentation; app-level security outcomes still require hands-on testing.
claimsRequiringTesting: Lovable can build a complete SaaS dashboard from one prompt.
Lovable reliably creates secure auth and role-based access.
Lovable generates correct Supabase RLS policies.
Lovable can build production-ready marketplace flows.
Lovable can safely integrate Stripe payments and webhooks.
Lovable fixes its own bugs without regressions.
Lovable-generated code is clean, maintainable, and easy for developers to extend.
Lovable apps perform well under realistic usage.
Lovable security scanner catches common app vulnerabilities.
Lovable is easier, faster, or more autonomous than competitors.

securityQuestions.Authentication: Does Lovable create secure signup, login, logout, password reset, session handling, and protected routes?
securityQuestions.Database rules: Does it create correct row-level security policies for every user-owned table?
securityQuestions.API keys: Does it avoid exposing secrets client-side and route sensitive calls through secure server-side functions?
securityQuestions.Environment variables: Are secrets environment-scoped, encrypted, and hidden from generated frontend code?
securityQuestions.Admin routes: Are admin pages protected server-side, not just hidden in the UI?
securityQuestions.Role-based access: Can user, admin, owner, client, and vendor permissions be bypassed through direct URLs or API calls?
securityQuestions.Payments/webhooks: Are payment webhooks verified server-side with idempotency, signature checks, and failure handling?
securityQuestions.Deployment settings: Are public/private publishing controls clear for each plan and project type?
securityQuestions.Error messages: Do generated apps leak stack traces, table names, keys, user IDs, or implementation details?
securityQuestions.Privacy: What user data is sent to Lovable, Supabase, AI models, analytics, or third-party services?

autonomyQuestions: Can it create a working app from a short prompt?
Can it refine a generated app from user feedback without breaking previous functionality?
Can it build multi-page flows with consistent navigation and state?
Can it create backend logic and database schema safely enough for realistic apps?
Can it diagnose and fix its own errors without regressions?
Can it deploy a usable live version with clear publish/update behavior?
Where does human review remain mandatory before launch?

testPlans.name: SaaS dashboard
testPlans.starterPrompt: Build a subscription analytics dashboard with auth, organizations, user roles, revenue charts, customer table, billing settings, and admin-only controls.
testPlans.whatToMeasure: Measure prompt-to-app completeness, schema quality, protected routes, dashboard state, role separation, and deploy behavior.

testPlans.name: Client portal
testPlans.starterPrompt: Build a client portal where clients can log in, view project status, upload files, message the team, approve deliverables, and see invoices.
testPlans.whatToMeasure: Measure auth flows, file permissions, client isolation, admin access, notifications, and data privacy.

testPlans.name: Marketplace MVP
testPlans.starterPrompt: Build a local services marketplace with vendor profiles, listings, search filters, booking requests, reviews, and admin moderation.
testPlans.whatToMeasure: Measure multi-role complexity, vendor/customer permissions, moderation, form validation, and workflow durability.

testPlans.name: Internal CRM
testPlans.starterPrompt: Build an internal CRM with lead stages, task assignments, notes, tags, saved filters, activity history, and manager-only reporting.
testPlans.whatToMeasure: Measure CRUD reliability, relational data model, permissions, auditability, search/filter usability, and realistic team workflow.

testPlans.name: AI content tool
testPlans.starterPrompt: Build an AI content planner with project folders, saved prompts, generated drafts, approval status, export options, and API-key-safe AI integration.
testPlans.whatToMeasure: Measure API key handling, prompt workflow quality, data persistence, revision history, and safe AI feature configuration.

comparisonCandidates.Bolt.new: browser-based app generation, backend/deployment workflow, code access, autonomy, security review
comparisonCandidates.Replit Agent: agentic full-stack building, hosting, debugging, database, logs, deployment
comparisonCandidates.Cursor: developer-centric AI coding versus Lovable chat/no-code workflow, control, maintainability, engineering fit
comparisonCandidates.v0 by Vercel: frontend/UI generation, React/Next.js workflow, design fidelity, handoff to developer deployment
comparisonCandidates.Bubble AI: visual no-code app building, database/workflow control, plugin ecosystem, production app maturity
comparisonCandidates.FlutterFlow AI: cross-platform/mobile app building, Firebase/Supabase integrations, visual development, export options
